summary: Cap title length in page_chunker._extract_title — single-line sources match the whole body as title
description:
**Bug:** `_extract_title` in `scripts/chunkers/page_chunker.py:30-43` searches the first 10 newline-split lines and matches `(.+?)` greedy — for sources that store an entire page (or hymn, or chapter) as a single line with no newlines, the whole content matches and becomes the "title".

**Repro:** any `chunking/<X>/<Y>.toml` with `strategy = "page-as-chunk"` whose raw text comes back from the source as one line. Verified for `chunking/greek_mystery/orphic-hymns.toml`.

**Fix sketch:**
- Cap candidate title length (e.g. skip lines > 80 chars).
- If no short line matches the pattern in the first 10, return None and let the caller format `"Hymn {n}"` (the `KeyError/IndexError` fallback at line 95 already handles that).
- Optional: add a sentence-boundary fallback that tries `re.split(r'\.\s+', content)` to find a candidate title within long lines.

**Test:** `tests/test_chunking.py` should grow a fixture for a single-line input that exercises the cap.

resolution.note: Default cap of 80 chars (configurable via title_max_len). 7 tests cover the regression, override, and edge cases. Single-line sources now safely return None instead of swallowing the body as title.
